Click on 'categories" at the task 'Set up categories and tags' 5. Add a category 6. Click on More features and enable Tags 7. Click on Tags tab and add some tags 8. Navigate to Concierge chat 9. title: [$250] Don't show Insights on Home if there is only one data point (e.g. month of data)

labels.name: External
labels.description: Added to denote the issue can be worked on by a contributor

labels.name: Daily
labels.description: KSv2

labels.name: Help Wanted
labels.description: Apply this label when an issue is open to proposals by contributors

labels.name: Bug
labels.description: Something is broken. Auto assigns a BugZero manager.

body:
I was recently doing some research and testing with the sign-up experience, and I came across this issue: - User recently signs-up - User creates an expense - We show a `Spend over time` graph with exactly one data point. Here's an example from a test account: Given that we have exactly one type of `Spend over time` graph on Home, which is grouped by month, I **think our solution is simply to not show `Spend over time` on Home until a user minimally has two months of data**. cc @puneetlath @shawnborton since we talked about this one. Also assigning to the original project team!
Issue OwnerCurrent Issue Owner: @truph01

If you haven’t already, check out our [contributing guidelines](https://github.com/Expensify/ReactNativeChat/blob/main/contributingGuides/CONTRIBUTING.md) for onboarding and email contributors@expensify.com to request to join our Slack channel! ___ **Version Number:** 9.3.55-0 **Reproducible in staging?:** Yes **Reproducible in production?:** Yes **If this was caught during regression testing, add the test name, ID and link from BrowserStack:** https://github.com/Expensify/App/pull/87034 **Email or phone of affected tester (no customers):** ibellicotest+2250@gmail.com **Issue reported by:** Applause Internal Team **Bug source:** Pull Request QA execution **Device used:** Motorola MotoG60 / Android 12 (Hybrid app) **App Component:** Notifications ## Action Performed: Prerequisite: Require two accounts in a policy, a submitter and a manager. (User A as submitter and User B as manager/owner) 1. Open the Expensify app as User A. 2. Open workspace chat where User B is manager. 3. Create a manual expense. 4. Open report of the just created expense and send a message. 5. Navigate away from report to "Inbox" 6. As User B, open the expense created by User A. 7. Tap on "Description" field, add a description and save. 8. As User A, note that a notification is received showing that manager just edited the description of the expense. ## Expected Result: Notifications shouldn´t be received for non-actionable actions on a report, such as the edition of description. ## Actual Result: Submitter of a workspace receives a notification after the manager edits the description of an expense. ## Workaround: Unknown ## Platforms: - [x] Android: App - [ ] Android: mWeb Chrome - [x] iOS: App - [ ] iOS: mWeb Safari - [ ] iOS: mWeb Chrome - [ ] Windows: Chrome - [ ] MacOS: Chrome / Safari ## Screenshots/Videos https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/14fb86f7-0e91-4b9e-8637-28c6fa54a550 [View all open jobs on GitHub](https://github.com/Expensify/App/issues?q=is%3Aopen+is%3Aissue+label%3A%22Help+Wanted%22)

title: [$250] Update the table and row styles for Mobile Reports page

labels.name: External
labels.description: Added to denote the issue can be worked on by a contributor

labels.name: Daily
labels.description: KSv2

labels.name: NewFeature
labels.description: Something to build that is a new item.

body:
We are in the process of updating the row/table styles for web. We also want to make similar changes for Mobile as well. The various list views on the Reports page suffer from a few problems: - They are inconsistent with Classic (Expenses list does not show status badge) - The font size for supporting elements is very tiny (11px) - They are visually very busy and hard to digest We’d like to propose some improvements to clean up the visual style and make these lists feel lighter, more modern, and easier to parse. Here’s what we’re thinking: - Bring status badges to the Expenses list - Bump small font sizes up to 13px - Remove action buttons and tags from rows to reduce visual clutter - Create a consistent template that can be used across the various row types found on Reports The result is this (Figma is [here](https://www.figma.com/design/DMwzhMmaL09kc8PayTEPun/NewDot-Compact-Styles?node-id=769-306165)): Individual mocks for easier inspectability: cc @Expensify/design @JmillsExpensify @trjExpensify @JS00001 @luacmartins
Issue OwnerCurrent Issue Owner: @Krishna2323

  • Upwork Job ID: 2042144127345066461

If you haven’t already, check out our [contributing guidelines](https://github.com/Expensify/ReactNativeChat/blob/main/contributingGuides/CONTRIBUTING.md) for onboarding and email contributors@expensify.com to request to join our Slack channel! ___ **Version Number:** V9.3.54-1 **Reproducible in staging?:** Yes **Reproducible in production?:** Yes **If this was caught during regression testing, add the test name, ID and link from BrowserStack:** https://test-management.browserstack.com/projects/2219752/test-runs/TR-16749/folder/40579099/88310091/1313414726 **Email or phone of affected tester (no customers):** dreamnitethedragon+vegst@gmail.com **Issue reported by:** Applause Internal Team **Bug source:** Regression TC Execution **Device used:** Redmi note 10s android 13 **App Component:** Left Hand Navigation (LHN) ## Action Performed: Pre-condition: Have 2 reports , 1 with payment status and 1 with approve status 1. Launch app 2.Open Home screen 3. Tap Begin next to pay 1 report 4. Note user directed to expense report 5. Tap approve in another report 6. Go to home 7. Tap pay 2 reports 8. Note user directed to reports - pay section conrrectly ## Expected Result: Tapping pay 1 report must direct to Report- Pay. ## Actual Result: Tapping pay 1 report directs to expense report. Step 14 fail in regression step. ## Workaround: Unknown ## Platforms: - [x] Android: App - [ ] Android: mWeb Chrome - [ ] iOS: App - [ ] iOS: mWeb Safari - [ ] iOS: mWeb Chrome - [x] Windows: Chrome - [ ] MacOS: Chrome / Safari ## Screenshots/Videos https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/61b6ad47-c65d-418d-81f1-eff4895bb40e [View all open jobs on GitHub](https://github.com/Expensify/App/issues?q=is%3Aopen+is%3Aissue+label%3A%22Help+Wanted%22)

If you haven’t already, check out our [contributing guidelines](https://github.com/Expensify/ReactNativeChat/blob/main/contributingGuides/CONTRIBUTING.md) for onboarding and email contributors@expensify.com to request to join our Slack channel! ___ **Version Number:** 9.3.54-1 **Reproducible in staging?:** Y **Reproducible in production?:** Y **If this was caught during regression testing, add the test name, ID and link from BrowserStack:** **Email or phone of affected tester (no customers):** **Logs:** https://stackoverflow.com/c/expensify/questions/4856 **Expensify/Expensify Issue URL:** **Issue reported by:** @dmkt9 **Slack conversation** (hyperlinked to channel name): [#Open Source](https://expensify.slack.com/archives/C01GTK53T8Q/p1775531205190389) ## Action Performed: Prerequisite: The user has several reports. 1. Open the application in a web browser 2. Navigate to Reports > Reports 3. Use the up/down arrow keys to navigate focus ## Expected Result: When at the top or bottom of the list, pressing the up or down arrow key should immediately focus the item at the opposite end of the list. ## Actual Result: The up/down arrow keys must be pressed multiple times before the focus returns to the top or bottom of the list. ## Workaround: Unknown ## Platforms: Select the officially supported platforms where the issue was reproduced: - [ ] Android: App - [ ] Android: mWeb Chrome - [ ] iOS: App - [ ] iOS: mWeb Safari - [ ] iOS: mWeb Chrome - [x] Windows: Chrome - [x] MacOS: Chrome / Safari ## Screenshots/Videos https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/dca64735-0f66-448c-ba8a-c7820120e2ea https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/77f26b9f-3b67-47a4-b463-7894c0ec3b09 [View all open jobs on GitHub](https://github.com/Expensify/App/issues?q=is%3Aopen+is%3Aissue+label%3A%22Help+Wanted%22)

body:
If you haven’t already, check out our [contributing guidelines](https://github.com/Expensify/ReactNativeChat/blob/main/contributingGuides/CONTRIBUTING.md) for onboarding and email contributors@expensify.com to request to join our Slack channel! ___ **Version Number:** 9.3.54-0 **Reproducible in staging?:** Yes **Reproducible in production?:** Yes **If this was caught during regression testing, add the test name, ID and link from BrowserStack:** https://test-management.browserstack.com/projects/2219752/test-runs/TR-16750/folder/13176791/59413485/1313415729 **Email or phone of affected tester (no customers):** ndk.2303+284@gmail.com **Issue reported by:** Applause Internal Team **Bug source:** Regression TC Execution **Device used:** Windows 11 Home/Chrome **App Component:** Money Requests ## Action Performed: 1. Open https://staging.new.expensify.com/ 2. Navigate to Workspace chat 3. Create a $10 expense 4. Open the expense details 5. Click on 'More > Split' 6. Split A to $3 7. Click 'Add Split' ## Expected Result: Verify a third split is added, A remains $3, B updates to $3.50, the new split C is added as $3.50 ## Actual Result: A third split is added, new split is added as $0 ## Workaround: Unknown ## Platforms: - [x] Android: App - [ ] Android: mWeb Chrome - [x] iOS: App - [ ] iOS: mWeb Safari - [ ] iOS: mWeb Chrome - [x] Windows: Chrome - [ ] MacOS: Chrome / Safari ## Screenshots/Videos https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/8c4508fd-9025-4847-9a83-57edcdccddbc [View all open jobs on GitHub](https://github.com/Expensify/App/issues?q=is%3Aopen+is%3Aissue+label%3A%22Help+Wanted%22)
